content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA of length NNN, we can define rotation as follows. If we rotate AAA to the right, all elements move to the right one unit, and the last element moves to the beginning. That is, it becomes [AN,A1,A2,…,AN−1][A_N, A_1,A_2,\ldots,A_{N-1}][AN​,A1​,A2​,…,AN−1​]. Similarly if we rotate AAA to the left, it becomes [A2,A3,…,AN,A1][A_2,A_3,\ldots,A_N, A_1][A2​,A3​,…,AN​,A1​].

Given an array AAA and an integer xxx, define f(A,x)f(A,x)f(A,x) to be the array AAA rotated by the amount xxx. If x≥0x\ge 0x≥0, this means we rotate it right xxx times. If x<0x<0x<0, this means we rotate it left ∣x∣|x|∣x∣ times.

You are given an array AAA of length NNN. Then QQQ queries follow. In each query, an integer xxx is given. To answer the query, you should replace AAA with A+f(A,x)A+f(A,x)A+f(A,x) where +++ denotes concatenation. After this operation, you must output the sum of all elements of AAA. Since this number can be large, output it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

Note that the queries are cumulative. When you modify AAA to answer one query, it starts that way for the next query.

###Input


The first line contains an integer NNN - the size of the initial array.
The second line contains NNN integers A1,…,ANA_1,\ldots, A_NA1​,…,AN​ - the elements of the initial array.
The third line contains an integer QQQ - the number of queries.
The fourth line contains QQQ space-separated integers x1,…,xQx_1,\ldots, x_Qx1​,…,xQ​, where xix_ixi​ is the parameter of the iii-th query.
###Output
After each query, output in a single line the the sum of all elements of the current array modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.

###Constraints


1≤N≤105 1\leq N\leq 10^51≤N≤105
1≤Q≤105 1\leq Q\leq 10^51≤Q≤105
−109≤Ai≤109 -10^9\leq A_i\leq 10^9−109≤Ai​≤109
−105≤xi≤105 -10^5 \leq x_i\leq 10^5−105≤xi​≤105
###Subtasks
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T – the number of test cases. For each test case:
- The first line contains an integer N – the size of the initial array.
- The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN.
- The third line contains an integer Q – the number of queries.
- The fourth line contains Q space‑separated integers x1 … xQ (the rotation amounts, which do not affect the answer).
For each query you must output the sum of the array after performing the operation described in the statement.
content.outputFormat: For each query of each test case output a single line containing the sum of all elements of the current array modulo 1000000007.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Initial sum = 1+2 = 3. After each query the sum doubles: 3*2 = 6, then 6*2 = 12.
Initial sum = 1-2+3 = 2. Doubling three times yields 4, 8 and 16.
Initial sum is 0; doubling 0 always stays 0.

solution.approach:
Rotating an array does not change the sum of its elements. After a query the array becomes A concatenated with a rotated copy of A, therefore the new sum is sum(A) + sum(rotated A) = 2·sum(A). Consequently each query simply doubles the current sum (mod 1e9+7) regardless of the value of x. The algorithm:
1. Compute the initial sum S of the given array and bring it into the range [0, MOD).
2. For each query: S = (S * 2) % MOD; output S.
The rotation amount is read only to consume the input.
Complexities: O(N + Q) time per test case, O(1) extra space.
